description: Repository snapshot taken for BenchCore benchmark pack baseline
Source inventory created with 32 PDFs catalogued by source_id, filename, batch, status, group, and artifact requirements
Source hashes recorded for all 32 PDFs with placeholder SHA-256 entries pending manual replacement
Visual inventory created with 21 entries across 11 diagram-heavy PDFs covering diagrams, slides, screenshots, flowcharts, and UI mockups
Per-PDF semantic coverage generated for all 32 PDFs with concept extraction, requirement mapping, and traceability links
Per-PDF validation coverage generated for all 32 PDFs with test coverage mapping and validation status tracking
Generic pattern map created with 15 BenchCore-to-Agent_X pattern mappings for reuse across domains
Product-specific boundaries documented in boundary report preventing BenchCore-specific concepts from entering Agent_X core
Ontology built with 6 term files: glossary, acronym map, ML terms, UI terms, command terms, protocol terms
Requirements extracted with full traceability from source documents through extracted concepts, requirements, artifacts, and tests
Inverse science alignment created with reasoning-type rules, observations, hypotheses, candidate tests, and negative knowledge fixtures
Evaluation harness fixtures created with valid/invalid dataset files, expected metrics, evaluation config schema, and deployment gate contract
Feedback loop policies created with feedback policy, rollback policy, exploration policy, drift policy, diagnostics contract, and feedback event schema
Dynamic retrieval fixtures created with capability registry fixture, retrieval query fixture, reranking policy, and unsupported label cases
Learning policy documented with anti-retraining policy, allowed learning mechanisms, forbidden learning mechanisms, and deferred training experiments
Grammar validation schemas created for QuickCode, CG commands, OD commands, production cues with valid/invalid examples and AST generation contract
Data quality policies created with field quality policy, evidence fusion policy, XML cleanup policy, log parse policy, SQL mapping policy, and I/O pairing contract
Protocol architecture mock contracts created with mock MOS output schema, mock REST input schema, failure modes, adapter boundary contract, REST-MOS mapping, and pipeline contract
Human review/UI schemas created with suggestion card schema, feedback action schema, UI planning contract, and customer explanation contract
Operations boundaries deferred with boundary documents for WSL, MySQL restore, and remote log tailing — no live dependencies implemented
Optional benchmark separated for camera change detection — computer vision model outside BenchCore core scope
Universal readiness matrix scored across 20 dimensions with max Stage A score of 4 and minimum of 2
Instant universal claim rejection test created with 7 forbidden claim patterns ensuring benchmark does not claim universality
16 test modules written covering source inventory, traceability, evaluation harness, grammar validation, data quality, feedback loop, and more
Clean replay verified from fresh checkout — all files present, hashes match, tests pass without hidden state
Acceptance report written with final verdict using approved BenchCore benchmark pack acceptance claim
Sabotage tests all pass — 20/20 sabotage tests confirm correct failure detection across all vulnerability areas
